name_en: Land cadastre of Ōga village, Namekata district, Hitachi Province
description_en: A kenchichō, a land cadastre for Ōga village in Namekata district of Hitachi Province. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (old document) by Itako City on 1987-07-14. Located in Ōga.

name_en: Artifacts excavated from the Nenemaizuka burial mound
description_en: Artifacts excavated from the Nenemaizuka Kofun burial mound. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (archaeological material) by Itako City on 1972-06-10. Located at Ushibori 17.

name_romaji: Ōei Shichi-nen Mei, Amida Sanzon Shuji Itabi
name_en: Itabi with Amida triad seed-syllables, dated Ōei 7 (1400)
description_en: An itabi (stone stupa tablet) carved with the seed-syllables (shuji) of the Amida triad and inscribed with the seventh year of the Ōei era (1400). Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (archaeological material) by Itako City on 1975-02-25. Located at Ueto 2154.
A haniwa terracotta figure representing an armored warrior. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (archaeological material) by Itako City on 1975-02-25. Located at Ushibori 175.
A Jōsō-type itabi (stone stupa tablet) at Egoji. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (archaeological material) by Itako City on 1992-05-01. Located in Egoji.
The Itako-bushi, a traditional folk song. Municipally designated as an intangible folk cultural property by Itako City on 1969-12-13. Associated with Itako.
The Miroku Odori, a traditional folk dance. Municipally designated as an intangible folk cultural property by Itako City on 1972-06-10. Performed in Tokushima.
The Tokushima Suijin Matsuri, a festival honoring the water deity. Municipally designated as an intangible folk cultural property by Itako City on 1995-10-25. Held in Tokushima.
The teijōsai, a courtyard ritual held at Ōu Shrine. Municipally designated as an intangible folk cultural property by Itako City on 1996-11-25. Held at Ōu 814.

name_en: Festival float figure of Minamoto no Yorimasa slaying the Nue
description_en: A dashi (festival float) figure depicting Minamoto no Yorimasa (Genzanmi) slaying the Nue monster. Municipally designated as a tangible folk cultural property by Itako City on 1976-12-10. Located in Itako 5-chōme.

name_en: Lion heads for the Furudaka sasara lion dance
description_en: Shishigashira (lion-dance heads) used in the sasara lion dance of Furudaka. Municipally designated as a tangible folk cultural property by Itako City on 1986-03-20. Located in Furudaka.

name_en: Festival float figure of Empress Jingū
description_en: A dashi (festival float) figure depicting Empress Jingū. Municipally designated as a tangible folk cultural property by Itako City on 1994-03-28. Located in Itako Hamachō.

name_romaji: Dashi Ningyō (Susanoo no Yamata-no-Orochi Taiji Okashira)
name_en: Festival float figure of Susanoo slaying the Yamata-no-Orochi
description_en: A dashi (festival float) figure depicting Susanoo-no-Mikoto slaying the eight-headed serpent Yamata-no-Orochi. Municipally designated as a tangible folk cultural property by Itako City on 2016-11-07. Located at Itako 995-1.

name_en: Festival float figure of Shizuka Gozen
description_en: A dashi (festival float) figure depicting Shizuka Gozen. Municipally designated as a tangible folk cultural property by Itako City on 2023-07-25. Located at Itako 856.
The ruins of Shimazaki Castle. Municipally designated as a monument (historic site) by Itako City on 1967-08-31. Located at Shimasu 530 and vicinity.
The grave of Miyamoto Chason. Municipally designated as a monument (historic site) by Itako City on 1969-12-13. Located at Itako 673.
The Ōu Tonozuka Kofun, an ancient burial mound. Municipally designated as a monument (historic site) by Itako City on 1969-12-13. Located at Ōu 804-3.

name_en: Grave site of the Ōu Tono family
description_en: The grave site of the Ōu Tono family. Municipally designated as a monument (historic site) by Itako City on 1969-12-13. Located at Ōu 750.

name_en: Site of the Nobukata village school
description_en: The site of the Nobukata gōkō (village school). Municipally designated as a monument (historic site) by Itako City on 1969-12-13. Located at Suka 3025.

name_romaji: Fudō no I, tsuketari Dōketsu, Fudō Myōō-zō
name_en: Fudō well, with cave and Fudō Myōō image
description_en: The Fudō well, together with an associated cave and an image of Fudō Myōō (Acala). Municipally designated as a monument (historic site) by Itako City on 1975-02-25. Located at Ueto 1334.

name_en: Site of the residence of Honma Jijun
description_en: The site of the villa (tei) of Honma Jijun. Municipally designated as a monument (historic site) by Itako City on 1976-12-10. Located at Itako 582.
The Omoi well (omoi ido) of Ōu. Municipally designated as a monument (historic site) by Itako City on 1976-12-10. Located at Ōu 751.
A jōyatō (everlasting stone lantern) at the Jizō river landing. Municipally designated as a monument (historic site) by Itako City on 1976-12-10. Located at Suzaki 388-ro.
A Jizō statue known as the Funakoshi Jizō at Kaidōji. Municipally designated as a monument (historic site) by Itako City on 1987-07-14. Located at Suzaki 849-1.

name_en: Eboshi-hanging pine of Magematsu Water Shrine
description_en: The eboshikake matsu, a pine associated with Magematsu Water Shrine. Municipally designated as a monument (historic site) by Itako City on 2022-02-28. Located at Magematsu-minami 645-1.
The ruins of Nagayama Castle (also written Nagayama). Municipally designated as a monument (historic site) by Itako City on 2022-12-23. Located at Nagayama, Shiroyama 1900 and vicinity.
A great ichō (ginkgo) tree at Mikumano Shrine. Municipally designated as a monument (natural monument) by Itako City on 1969-08-01. Located at Ushibori 175.
A great shii (chinquapin) tree at Mogi. Municipally designated as a monument (natural monument) by Itako City on 1969-08-01. Located at Mogi 505.
A maki (podocarpus) tree at Chōkoku-ji temple. Municipally designated as a monument (natural monument) by Itako City on 1969-08-01. Located at Ueto 166.
A kaya (Japanese nutmeg-yew) tree at Myōkō-ji temple. Municipally designated as a monument (natural monument) by Itako City on 1972-06-10. Located at Tsukiji 601.
A harigiri (castor aralia) tree at Tanomori. Municipally designated as a monument (natural monument) by Itako City on 1972-06-10. Located at Mizuhara 1988-1.
A great ichō (ginkgo) tree at Itako. Municipally designated as a monument (natural monument) by Itako City on 1972-06-10. Located at Itako 442.

name_en: Sacred grove of Hiyoshi Sannō Shrine
description_en: The wooded grove of Hiyoshi Sannō Shrine. Municipally designated as a monument (natural monument) by Itako City on 1975-02-25. Located at Nagayama 1176.
A maki (podocarpus) tree at Nihonmatsu-ji temple. Municipally designated as a monument (natural monument) by Itako City on 1975-02-25. Located at Horinouchi 1230.
A hiyokuhiba (Hinoki cypress cultivar) at Shimizu. Municipally designated as a monument (natural monument) by Itako City on 1975-02-25. Located at Shimizu 467.
A tabu (machilus) tree at Shimizu. Municipally designated as a monument (natural monument) by Itako City on 1975-02-25. Located at Shimizu 467.
The jusō (wooded grove) of Kashima Yoshida Shrine. Municipally designated as a monument (natural monument) by Itako City on 1976-12-10. Located at Shingū 1885.
A bodaiju (linden) tree at Chōshō-ji temple. Municipally designated as a monument (natural monument) by Itako City on 1976-12-10. Located at Itako 428.

name_en: Camphor tree of the Nobukata Community Center
description_en: A kusunoki (camphor) tree at the Nobukata Community Center. Municipally designated as a monument (natural monument) by Itako City on 1995-10-25. Located at Shingū 1493-2.
A great keyaki (Japanese zelkova) tree at Nobukata. Municipally designated as a monument (natural monument) by Itako City on 1995-10-25. Located at Shingū 1929-2.
